description: Events in this allowlist are suppressed from PCDoctor report findings. Still counted in metrics.event_errors_7d totals so trending detects if suppressed event counts spike abnormally. Add new entries as {provider, event_id, reason, max_7d, max_30d} -- if observed count exceeds the corresponding max, the event will still be raised as a warning (anomaly even though allowlisted).

allowlist.reason: PPL-protected sppsvc.exe access-denied messages. Harmless. Investigated 2026-04-15 with Procmon.
DCOM server slow-register timing. Almost always iTunes Helper / Office click-to-run / background app registering late.
DCOM server activation failure -- usually benign third-party component.
Service failed to start -- usually third-party transient (Logitech/NVIDIA/update agents).
Service terminated unexpectedly -- usually third-party transient.
Minifilter attach failure on transient mounts (USB/NAS/OneDrive). Expected on this system.
Individual update package failed -- Windows Update retries automatically.
Universal Print per-print-job evaluation event -- normal.
Known harmless Win11 noise: QueryStateContainerItemInfo ApiSet returning 0x800700A8. Microsoft telemetry churn.
NetBIOS WORKGROUP name contention (second host on LAN). Harmless; disable NetBT over TCP/IP if desired.
Endpoint discovery failure for transient Bluetooth/USB devices. Harmless.

Microsoft Gaming Services crash-loop dominates 7034 on this box. Tried Remove-AppxPackage + reinstall (2026-04-30) -- Store re-stages and the loop returns. Sandboxed; cosmetic event-log noise only. If observed count spikes past 180/7d, a NEW service may have joined the crash pool -- investigate.
